description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-14764
 
 Published : July 5, 2026, 5:17 p.m. | 27minutes ago
 
 Description : A vulnerability has been found in code-projects Hotel and Tourism Reservation 1.0. This impacts an unknown function of the file /admin/add_event.php of the component Event Management Page. Such manipulation of the argument fdetails leads to sql injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

title: CVE-2026-14761 - radareorg radare2 str.c r_str_append integer overflow
description:
CVE ID : CVE-2026-14761
 
 Published : July 5, 2026, 4:19 p.m. | 1hour, 25minutes ago
 
 Description : A security vulnerability has been detected in radareorg radare2 up to 6.1.6. The affected element is the function r_str_ndup/r_str_append of the file libr/util/str.c. The manipulation leads to integer overflow. An attack has to be approached locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The identifier of the patch is a20a56917ae85d732e683f8d9078bdcfee92446c. Apply

A newly released open-source security framework called T3MP3ST is turning general-purpose AI coding agents like Claude Code, OpenAIs Codex, and Hermes into autonomous red-teaming operators without requiring new API keys, cloud infrastructure, or additional billing. title: CVE-2025-71347: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect malicious pickle files using numpy.f2py.crackfortran.param_eval function in red
description: picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect malicious pickle files using numpy.f2py.crackfortran.param_eval function in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling arbitrary code execution in applications loading untrusted pickle data.

title: CVE-2026-54424: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Pri
description: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Privilege. This issue affects Parsec through v2026-05-04.0. The patched version isParsec for Windows version 150-104a. A user can generate a situation where there is an instance of parsecd.exe running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with a user-controlled value of the AppData environment variable.
